description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly light gray coloration, speckled with darker mineral inclusions throughout its surface. Its form is elongated and somewhat wedge-like, with one end tapering to a narrower point and the other end broader and more angular. The surface texture appears rough and granular, with visible small pits and unevenness typical of natural stone. There are no visible signs of artificial shaping or carving, suggesting it is a naturally occurring fragment. The stone's coloration and speckling pattern are consistent across all sides, with no significant discoloration or moss growth. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a minor obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or along a sidewalk.
description: A rough, elongated light gray stone with dark speckles and a granular, uneven surface. Its natural, unshaped form makes it suitable as an urban obstacle or decorative feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears elongated and flat, with a broad, slightly curved surface and visible speckling.
description_view_1: This view shows the stone's narrow, tapered edge and highlights its thickness and rough, uneven sides.
description_view_2: The broader end of the stone is visible, showing a more angular and blocky profile with consistent speckling.
description_view_3: The stone's end is seen here, revealing its irregular, chunky cross-section and rough texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled speckling of dark and light minerals, irregular and natural with no artificial markings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and granular, with minor surface pitting indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil or gravel, may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well in landscaped areas.
